notes: The --model-PINNED variant of 1.8: launch gemini with -m gemini-3-pro-preview (a first-class served, non-default model vs the flash default) and after one no-op turn the dashboard shows ModelName = the pinned launch model (not 'unknown') and ContextWindow matches that model's published window. gemini-cli carries everything in the *.jsonl transcript the daemon tails: the gemini assistant message's top-level model -> ModelName (the pinned model; parseAssistant reads raw.model identically regardless of how the model was selected, and NormalizeModelName leaves gemini ids unchanged), the per-message tokens block -> TotalTokens, and ContextWindow from the capacity table for the resolved gemini model -> ComputeContextUtilization > 0. The phase DSL asserts the basic-turn state arc; observations assert the model/cost/token vector. observations.model is PROVISIONAL: the only captured fixture shows the default 'gemini-3-flash-preview', so the exact string recorded for -m gemini-3-pro-preview is settled by record (a different live string is a string update, not a daemon bug). context_window and cost are provisional on the LiteLLM cache carrying gemini-3-pro-preview at record time.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after launch
Launcher PID bound by cwd shortly after launch

Turn completes back to ready; SessionMetrics.ModelName is the pinned launch model (not 'unknown') and ContextWindow > 0 (window from the capacity table for the resolved gemini model); state never entered waiting
